skill.name: setting-up-sending-domain
skill.description: Use when adding or verifying a Mailtrap sending domain, DNS propagation issues, registrar or DNS provider steps, compliance after verification, or click tracking. Domain must be verified before sending from it.
skill.summary: Step-by-step guide for adding, verifying, and troubleshooting Mailtrap sending domains with DNS configuration.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate documentation skill for setting up Mailtrap sending domains. All static findings are false positives: markdown backticks were misidentified as Ruby/shell execution, documentation URLs flagged as network risks, and DKIM references misclassified as weak cryptography. The only real network and command usage is standard DNS verification via `dig`/`nslookup` and API calls to mailtrap.io.

medium_findings.title: Documentation references DNS lookup commands
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md references `dig` and `nslookup` on line 51 for DNS verification troubleshooting. These are standard, read-only DNS query tools used for legitimate domain verification. No execution risk exists as this is documentation, not executable code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static flagged as reconnaissance but context shows standard DNS troubleshooting commands documented for users to run manually.

low_findings.title: API endpoints documented for programmatic domain management
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents GET/POST endpoints at mailtrap.io for sending domain management. These are official API references for legitimate automation workflows, not unauthorized network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Endpoints are documented Mailtrap API calls requiring Bearer token auth, clearly intended for legitimate domain setup automation.

low_findings.title: Documentation links to DNS provider guides
low_findings.description: Multiple hardcoded URLs point to Mailtrap documentation pages for Cloudflare, AWS Route 53, Google Cloud DNS, and other providers. These are reference links for users, not active network connections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static flagged hardcoded URLs but these are documentation reference links to official Mailtrap guides, not exfiltration or C2 endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown backticks (used for inline code formatting) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are documentation formatting, not executable code. Found throughout SKILL.md.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Reviewing the file content confirms these are standard markdown inline code formatting, not actual code execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: DKIM references misidentified as weak cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'DKIM' (DomainKeys Identified Mail) as a weak cryptographic algorithm. DKIM is a standard email authentication protocol, not a cryptographic weakness. The skill documents proper email authentication setup.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: DKIM is an email authentication standard, not a cryptographic vulnerability. False positive from keyword matching.

content.user_title: Set Up and Verify Mailtrap Sending Domains
content.value_statement: Configuring DNS records for email sending is complex and error-prone. This skill provides clear guidance for adding, verifying, and troubleshooting Mailtrap sending domains across major DNS providers.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through adding and verifying Mailtrap sending domains
Explain DNS record requirements (SPF, DKIM, MX) for email authentication
Provide provider-specific setup steps for Cloudflare, Route 53, Google Cloud DNS, GoDaddy, Namecheap, and DigitalOcean
Troubleshoot DNS propagation issues using dig and nslookup commands
Document Mailtrap Sending Domains API endpoints for programmatic setup
Explain proxied DNS caveats that affect verification records
limitations: Does not cover sandbox-only email testing (use the testing-with-sandbox skill instead)
Cannot perform DNS changes directly - guides users to their provider's interface or API
Requires a verified Mailtrap account and API token for programmatic workflows
DNS propagation times depend on the registrar and are outside Mailtrap's control

use_cases.title: New domain setup for production sending
use_cases.description: Developer needs to configure a custom domain for transactional emails and requires step-by-step DNS configuration guidance.

use_cases.title: Troubleshooting stuck domain verification
use_cases.description: DevOps engineer needs to diagnose why domain verification is pending despite adding DNS records hours ago.

use_cases.title: Automated domain provisioning in CI/CD
use_cases.description: Platform team wants to script domain creation and DNS record publishing across multiple environments using the Mailtrap API.

prompt_templates.title: Add a new sending domain
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add a new sending domain to my Mailtrap account. The domain I want to use is example.com. Walk me through the steps to add it in the Mailtrap UI and configure the required DNS records.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot verification failure
prompt_templates.prompt: My sending domain verification has been pending for over 24 hours. I added all the DNS records shown in the Mailtrap UI. How can I diagnose the issue and get verification to complete?

prompt_templates.title: Configure subdomain for sending
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to send emails from notifications.mycompany.com. Should I add mycompany.com or notifications.mycompany.com as the sending domain? Walk me through the subdomain setup process.

prompt_templates.title: Automate DNS record publishing via API
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to automate sending domain creation and DNS record publishing in our infrastructure pipeline. Show me how to use the Mailtrap Sending Domains API to create a domain and then push the DNS records to Cloudflare using their API.

output_examples.input: I added my domain but verification is still pending after 2 hours. What should I check?
output: Confirm each DNS record is published using dig or nslookup:
Check TXT record for SPF: dig TXT example.com
Check CNAME for DKIM: dig CNAME mailtrap._domainkey.example.com
Verify records resolve to the exact values shown in the Mailtrap UI
Wait for DNS propagation (can take up to 48 hours)
Click Verify in the Mailtrap UI once records are visible

output_examples.input: Should I use a subdomain or root domain for sending?
output: Use the exact hostname you will use in the From address
If sending from notifications.mycompany.com, add that subdomain
Only add mycompany.com if you truly send from the root domain
Add the complete set of DNS records as shown in the UI
Do not cherry-pick records - all are required for verification

best_practices: Add the complete set of DNS records exactly as shown in the Mailtrap UI - do not cherry-pick
Use DNS-only mode (grey cloud) for verification records on Cloudflare, not proxied orange cloud
Wait for DNS propagation before clicking Verify - use dig or nslookup to confirm public visibility
Store MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN securely in environment variables, never in code repositories
anti_patterns: Adding only some DNS records instead of the complete set provided by Mailtrap
Using proxied (orange cloud) DNS for SPF/DKIM records on Cloudflare
Clicking Verify immediately after adding records without checking propagation
Hardcoding API tokens in scripts or committing them to version control

faq.question: Why is my domain verification still pending?
faq.answer: DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours. Use dig or nslookup to confirm each record is publicly visible with the exact values from the Mailtrap UI. If records are correct but verification fails, check that verification records are not proxied on Cloudflare.

faq.question: Should I add a subdomain or root domain?
faq.answer: Add the exact hostname you will use in the From address. If you send from notifications.example.com, add that subdomain. Only add the root domain if you truly send from it.

faq.question: Can I automate sending domain setup?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the Mailtrap Sending Domains API to list, create, and check verification status. You can then publish DNS records using your provider's API (Cloudflare, Route 53, etc.) or Infrastructure as Code tools.

faq.question: What API token permissions do I need?
faq.answer: You need a Mailtrap API token with access to the Sending Domains endpoints. Token scope, storage, and account ID resolution are covered in the authorizing-api-requests skill.

faq.question: Do I need to complete compliance after verification?
faq.answer: Yes, if Mailtrap requests it after verification. The compliance step is required before you can send from the domain in production.

faq.question: Is this skill only for sandbox testing?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for live sending domains. For sandbox-only testing without a custom domain, use the testing-with-sandbox skill instead.
